title: Add strict per-command flag and positional validation to bench before any side-effecting dispatch
problem: Generic parseArgs accepts every --flag, and most dispatch arms never reject unused flags. Unsupported safety flags such as --dry-run and unknown flags such as --jsno can be silently dropped while detached processes launch.
evidence: ui/bench.ts:79-92
ui/bench.ts:992-1018
probe: bun ui/bench.ts eval-run --suite evals/suites/smoke.json --dry-run exited 0 and launched a harness
implementation_hint: Derive allowed flags and arity from command metadata or a new command schema. Reject unknown flags and unexpected positionals with bench-error.v1 JSON before calling startEvalRun/startOptimizeRun/startRun.

title: Expose a safe eval-run robot path through the primary bench CLI
problem: Agents naturally try --dry-run on eval-run, but bench currently ignores it and starts the live detached harness. The direct runner has a good --robot-dry-run path that the primary CLI does not surface or forward.
evidence: probe: bench eval-run --dry-run launched a detached process
harness/runner/run_eval.py:76-85 defines dry-run/json-summary/robot-dry-run
ui/bench.ts:992-1000 forwards only suite/case/arm
implementation_hint: Support bench eval-run --robot-dry-run/--dry-run/--replay/--json-summary by calling the runner synchronously, or reject those flags with a precise JSON error and next_command pointing at uv run harness/runner/run_eval.py --robot-dry-run.
tests: new bench eval-run robot dry-run contract test

title: Add did-you-mean recovery for unknown bench commands and flags
problem: Unknown commands return JSON usage but no correction. Unknown flags should become structured errors with suggestions once strict validation lands.
evidence: ui/bench.ts:1061-1066
probe: bun ui/bench.ts evalrun ... returned no did_you_mean
implementation_hint: Use edit distance and prefix matching against COMMAND_DETAILS. Include did_you_mean and next_command fields in bench-error.v1.
tests: unknown command typo tests for evalrun/evalruns/workflowrun

title: Make runner robot-mode failures JSON from argument parsing through suite loading
problem: The success path emits eval-dry-run-summary.v1, but argparse conflicts, unknown flags, suite errors, and empty matrices are prose. Stored output cannot be consumed uniformly by agents.
evidence: harness/runner/run_eval.py:69-114
harness/runner/run_eval.py:117-136
probe: --json-summary --badflag emitted argparse prose
implementation_hint: Add a parser/error wrapper for --robot-dry-run or any argv containing --json-summary. Emit eval-error.v1 with phase, exit_code, message, and suggested_command.

title: Give gen_eval_cases validate/promote stable JSON schemas and clearer status semantics
problem: Validate-only emits ad hoc JSON without schema_version, and ok:true can coexist with sensitivity_status:fail. Promote returns mixed prose/JSON and lacks a schema contract.
evidence: harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py:669-682
probe: validate-only returned ok true with sensitivity_status fail
implementation_hint: Define case-validation.v1 and case-promote-result.v1 schemas. Include aggregate ok, per-gate statuses, exit_code, and whether sensitivity failures are advisory or blocking.
tests: schema validation tests for validate-only and promote dry failures

title: Harden promote with rollback-on-exception, atomic suite writes, and bounded post-checks
problem: Promote mutates case directories and suite files, but rollback is problem-driven rather than exception-safe, and post-check subprocesses can hang indefinitely.
evidence: subagent finding: gen_eval_cases.py:584-619
subagent finding: gen_eval_cases.py:604-609
implementation_hint: Wrap mutation phase in try/except/finally, write suite updates through temp file plus atomic replace, and pass a timeout to post-promote check subprocesses.

title: Enrich repo-check-result.v1 with next_commands, exit_code, and failure kind
problem: Check scripts are parseable but under-prescriptive. Agents must infer whether a JSON fail is a usage error or validation debt, and they do not receive a next command.
evidence: scripts/checklib.py:72-118
subagent finding: argparse JSON payload has status fail but no kind
implementation_hint: Add optional fields while preserving repo-check-result.v1 compatibility, or version to repo-check-result.v2. Include validation_failure versus usage_error and suggested rerun commands.

title: Improve detached-process recovery metadata and status filtering
problem: Detached launch responses and eval-runs output can be too volatile or large for agent loops. eval-runs returned a very large payload during audit, and live launch success can precede asynchronous spawn failure.
evidence: ui/lib.ts:1556-1578 sidecar includes pid and startedAtMs
subagent finding: detached spawn errors may report success
probe: eval-runs payload was large enough to require filtering
implementation_hint: Preflight executables before success, include next_commands, add eval-runs --running/--limit/--json-lines filters, and mark volatile fields in capabilities.

title: Bound and filter bench eval-runs output for agent loops
problem: The primary run-listing command can return too much historical run data for an agent status loop, and agents lack a compact running-only or line-delimited stream.
evidence: subagent finding: eval-runs payload was large enough to require filtering
probe: bun ui/bench.ts eval-runs had no --running/--limit/--json-lines before pass 3
implementation_hint: Add --status, --running, --limit, and --json-lines, return a schema-versioned bounded envelope, and document next commands.

title: Accept top-level help and common global machine-mode flags
problem: Agents commonly start with --help or prefix commands with --json, but bench rejected top-level --help and treated --json as a command even though output is already JSON.
evidence: subagent finding: bun ui/bench.ts --help failed as unknown command
subagent finding: bun ui/bench.ts --json doctor failed despite JSON default
implementation_hint: Special-case top-level --help and strip global no-op flags before command dispatch while advertising them in capabilities.

title: Support --flag=value in generic and bespoke bench parsers
problem: Several agent and shell generators emit --flag=value, but bench only supported separated --flag value syntax for many options.
evidence: subagent finding: --project=. and similar inline flags were rejected
probe: eval-case-generate needed bespoke parser support
implementation_hint: Normalize long options through a shared parser helper, preserve duplicate-scalar checks, and reject inline values on boolean flags.

title: Standardize bench-owned errors on bench-error.v1
problem: Bench-owned command and validation failures used inconsistent JSON shapes, making retry and status handling harder for agents.
evidence: subagent finding: inconsistent CLI error envelopes
probe: unknown command error lacked schema_version/status/exit_code
implementation_hint: Emit bench-error.v1 with status and exit_code for bench-owned errors, while preserving wrapped subprocess schemas that are already more specific.

title: Accept a skill directory path for first-run eval-case generation
problem: The natural first command for a brand-new skill is to pass its filesystem path, but gen_eval_cases.py previously only accepted a repo-local skill name under skills/<name>. New skills with no evals therefore required a separate import/onboarding step before case generation could even start.
evidence: harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py:920
probe expectation: uv run harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py --skill /path/to/skill --n 3 should import/use the skill and enter bootstrap context
implementation_hint: Resolve --skill before SkillContext as either a repo skill name or a full skill directory path. Import the full directory into skills/<frontmatter-name> when the repo copy is missing.

title: Infer bootstrap context for true zero-case generation
problem: Pass 4 preserved bootstrap for validate/promote, but generation itself still risked failing before the LM path with 'no loadable eval cases to seed from' unless the agent knew to add --bootstrap.
evidence: harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py:920-923
tests/test_gen_eval_cases_cli_contract.py:test_generation_infers_bootstrap_for_external_zero_case_skill_path
implementation_hint: Treat a skill with no visible eval case directories as bootstrap context for generation, validate-only, and promote, unless a seed example is explicitly requested.

title: Import the full skill directory and record provenance
problem: A skill is not just SKILL.md; references, schemas, scripts, and assets can be required to bootstrap credible eval cases. Path import also needs provenance so agents can tell which repo skill name was generated from which source path.
evidence: user clarification: skill can have supporting directories; better primary path is skill directory
harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py:116-174
implementation_hint: Copy the full skill directory, ignore only obvious local/build junk, accept SKILL.md file paths only as an alias for the parent directory, and include source/import metadata in generation config/report.

title: Advertise the first-run name-or-path workflow through bench help and docs
problem: Even if the generator supports skill paths, agents will keep using only --skill <name> if capabilities/help/docs do not surface the path form and zero-case bootstrap behavior.
evidence: ui/bench.ts command metadata
README.md eval-case generation block
ui/README.md command examples
implementation_hint: Change eval-case-generate usage to --skill <name-or-path>, document skill-directory path as the primary path form, and note that SKILL.md is only an alias for its parent directory.

title: Synthesize a minimal Laguna contract for imported prompt-style skills
problem: Real skills under ~/.agents/skills, including philip, can have scripts and references but no Laguna schemas or validate_*.ts validators. Path import worked, but the generator previously stopped instead of adding the missing harness layer and continuing.
evidence: real probe: /home/ben/.agents/skills/philip imports with Workflows/, docs/, scripts/, then lacks scripts/validate_*.ts
harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py:238-361
implementation_hint: For path-imported skills lacking validators, generate a minimal .laguna/<skill>.json output contract, JSON schema, and validate_<skill>_synthetic_bootstrap.ts on the imported repo copy before loading SkillContext.

title: Report LM setup failures without Python tracebacks
problem: After synthetic Laguna bootstrap, a missing API key can still surface as a raw RuntimeError traceback, which makes the first-run flow look crashed instead of waiting for model credentials or validate/promote inputs.
evidence: real philip probe with --api-key-env __GEN_EVAL_CASES_REAL_SMOKE_MISSING_KEY__ previously leaked a Traceback
harness/generate/gen_eval_cases.py:1126
implementation_hint: Catch make_lm RuntimeError during generation setup and print a concise stderr error plus next actions.

title: Create gated no-LM starter cases for first-run bootstrap
problem: A real external prompt-style skill such as philip can import and synthesize a Laguna contract, but if no LM key is configured the first run still ends before creating any reviewable candidate artifacts.
evidence: probe: gen_eval_cases.py --skill /home/ben/.agents/skills/philip --n 1 --api-key-env __GEN_EVAL_CASES_PASS6_MISSING_KEY__ returned a clean setup error but no candidates
Pass 6 smoke: --no-lm-skeleton produced one candidate with replay_status pass and sensitivity_status fail
implementation_hint: Add an explicit no-LM skeleton generation mode and use it automatically for bootstrap contexts when LM setup fails. Write normal runs/generate candidates, config, report, and promote hints.

title: Exclude external local state from skill path imports
problem: Full-directory path import preserved support files, but the real philip import also copied local state directories such as .beads/ and agent_ergonomics_audit/, which are not part of the skill contract and can pollute repo checks.
evidence: Pass 6 real philip import listed skills/philip/.beads and skills/philip/agent_ergonomics_audit before fix
tests/test_gen_eval_cases_cli_contract.py imports prompt-style fixture with .beads and agent_ergonomics_audit and asserts they are excluded
implementation_hint: Extend the import ignore list to skip local state and run-output directories while preserving SKILL.md, references, docs, scripts, schemas, and other contract/support files.

title: Expose no-LM bootstrap through bench and docs
problem: After adding an offline generator mode, the normal agent-facing bench wrapper still rejected --no-lm-skeleton as unknown and help/docs did not teach the path.
evidence: ui/bench.ts eval-case-generate flag allowlist lacked no-lm-skeleton
Pass 6 bench smoke: bun ui/bench.ts eval-case-generate --skill /home/ben/.agents/skills/philip --no-lm-skeleton returned bench-eval-case-generate.v1
implementation_hint: Add --no-lm-skeleton to command metadata, bespoke parser, argv forwarding, tests, README, ui README, and credential/offline docs.

title: Reuse an existing repo copy for repeated external skill path invocations
problem: After an external skill path had been imported once, repeating the same path failed with an 'already exists' error and told the agent to switch to --skill <name>, even though the intent was legible and safe to satisfy by using the existing repo copy.
evidence: Pass 6 ce-plan smoke: bench eval-case-generate against the original ce-plan path failed after direct import because skills/ce-plan already existed
implementation_hint: When a path resolves to a skill name whose repo copy already exists, use that repo copy and report skill_import_reused=true rather than requiring the agent to rewrite the command.

title: Avoid deterministic starter dedup failures after a skeleton case is promoted
problem: A repeated no-LM skeleton generation after promoting the starter reused the same case id and input bytes, so mechanical dedup rejected the otherwise valid second starter.
evidence: Pass 6 ce-plan smoke: second bench retry returned dedup violations for ce-plan-no-lm-bootstrap-starter and byte-identical input/brief.md
implementation_hint: Choose a unique skeleton case id when the base id already exists and include the case id in the starter input fixture so byte-level dedup has a real distinction.

title: Fallback on call-time LM authentication/provider failures during bootstrap
problem: The bootstrap fallback handled missing key setup before LM creation, but real default provider auth failed only when the first LiteLLM call was made. That leaked a Python traceback after importing the external skill and left the agent with no starter candidate.
evidence: Pass 6 ce-plan e2e: default generation without Anthropic credentials imported skills/ce-plan, then litellm.AuthenticationError escaped during spec proposal
retry after fix: same command exited 0, warned once, and produced a gated no-LM starter candidate
implementation_hint: Catch LM exceptions around spec proposal and case materialization calls in bootstrap context and route them to the same no-LM skeleton generator; keep non-bootstrap failures concise with next actions.

title: Normalize imported prompt-style skills for GEPA structure gates
problem: A real external prompt-style skill could import, bootstrap cases, and run evals, but GEPA smoke rejected the imported copy because it lacked repo-required `metadata.version` and a non-goals heading.
evidence: Pass 6 ce-plan GEPA bridge smoke initially failed with frontmatter-version and skill-non-goals-section violations
After import normalization, `gepa_skill.py --skill ce-plan --smoke` returned ok true
implementation_hint: When path-importing an external skill, patch only the repo-local imported copy with minimal `metadata.version` and an appended `## Do not use when` section before synthetic Laguna/GEPA use. Do not mutate the external source and do not put the non-goals section before the primary skill instructions.

title: Allow multi-promote batches to complete a partial bootstrap corpus
problem: After one starter case was promoted, promoting the next two cases in one command failed one-by-one because each intermediate state still violated corpus minimum/adversarial checks, even though the full batch would complete the corpus for GEPA.
evidence: Pass 6 ce-plan GEPA bridge: batch promoting second and adversarial cases rolled back on skill-min-cases/skill-adversarial-case before fix
After fix, the two-case batch promoted successfully and created a three-case suite with a train/val split
implementation_hint: For multi-promote, validate intermediate candidates with single-case checks and reserve full corpus checks for the final candidate, preserving rollback for real errors.

title: Let GEPA reflection use the authenticated pool agent selector path
problem: The optimizer could run evals through pool but reflection required a LiteLLM/OpenRouter credential path. When OPENROUTER_API_KEY was absent after repo movement, agents could not use the same authenticated pool model-selector path that the rest of the workbench already exposes.
evidence: Pass 6 functional ce-plan continuation: zsh and process env reported OPENROUTER_API_KEY missing
Direct pool probe with anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5 returned successfully
GEPA run pass6-functional-gepa-pool-haiku reached reflection/proposal through pool
implementation_hint: Add --reflection-pool-agent to gepa_skill.py and bench optimize-skill, shelling out to pool exec with prompt-file traces under the optimize run directory.

title: Clean pool reflection transcripts before GEPA candidate extraction
problem: Pool markdown output can contain trajectory text, tool-call transcripts, and earlier fenced shell output. GEPA then extracted the wrong fenced block as the replacement skill text, producing a candidate that scored 0 before any useful held-out comparison.
evidence: pass6-functional-gepa-pool-haiku: first pool proposal contained grep/read/tool transcript blocks before the final candidate
clean_pool_reflection_output selected a frontmatter-bearing fenced block and wrote reflection-pool/call-*/cleaned.md
implementation_hint: Prompt pool reflection as a no-tools direct answer and return the best candidate-looking fenced block, preferring SKILL.md frontmatter, while preserving raw stdout/stderr for postmortem.

title: Use calibrated external-skill cases with non-perfect seed scores before claiming skill-performance uplift
problem: The first ce-plan GEPA bridge used synthetic starter cases where the seed scored 1.0, so the end-to-end loop proved wiring but could not show actual skill lift. A functional use case needs correct gold artifacts plus seed failures.
evidence: pass6-functional-three-case-baseline: held-out ce-plan seed score 0.85 with failures on evidence_sources/title
pass6-functional-gepa-pool-reflection: total_metric_calls 4, best_val_score 0.85, best_changed false
pass6-functional-gepa-pool-haiku-contract-feedback: total_metric_calls 4, best_val_score 0.85, best_changed false
implementation_hint: Generate or curate realistic prompt-style cases from real repository plans, validate/promote enough train/val/adversarial cases, and record actual seed/best scores separately from agent-ergonomics uplift.
tests: live gepa_skill.py baseline-only and optimizer smoke

title: Strip synthetic bootstrap contracts from GEPA seeds when optimizing against real validators
problem: External prompt-style imports need synthetic Laguna scaffolding for first-run bootstrap, but that scaffolding can contaminate later GEPA runs on calibrated non-synthetic suites. The reflector can optimize toward the generated synthetic schema instead of the prompt-local real contract.
evidence: OpenRouter ce-plan run 20260616-211543Z proposed guidance around ce-plan.synthetic-bootstrap.v1 while the functional cases expected .laguna/ce-plan.json plan-quality artifacts
pass6-clean-seed-smoke config recorded seed_sanitization and disallowed ce-plan.synthetic-bootstrap.v1
pass6-clean-gepa-openrouter-short reached 4/8 calls with sanitizer active; candidate tied train score 0.7 and was skipped, with no accepted skill-performance uplift
implementation_hint: Detect whether active suite validators are synthetic-bootstrap-only. If not, strip the generated Synthetic Laguna Bootstrap Contract section from the seed component and reject candidates that reintroduce synthetic schema literals.
tests: live gepa_skill.py smoke/baseline with sanitized config evidence
